speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thus sometimes hath the brightest day a cloud,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And after summer evermore succeeds

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Barren winter, with his wrathful nipping cold;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: So cares and joys abound, as seasons fleet.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sirs, what’s o’clock?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Ten, my lord.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ten is the hour that was appointed me

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To watch the coming of my punished duchess.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Uneath may she endure the flinty streets,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To tread them with her tender-feeling feet.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sweet Nell, ill can thy noble mind abrook

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The abject people gazing on thy face

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With envious looks laughing at thy shame,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That erst did follow thy proud chariot wheels

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: When thou didst ride in triumph through the streets.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But, soft! I think she comes, and I’ll prepare

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My tearstained eyes to see her miseries.

speaker: SERVANT
text: So please your Grace, we’ll take her from the Sheriff.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: No, stir not for your lives. Let her pass by.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Come you, my lord, to see my open shame?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Now thou dost penance too. Look how they gaze!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: See how the giddy multitude do point,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And nod their heads, and throw their eyes on thee.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, Gloucester, hide thee from their hateful looks,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And, in thy closet pent up, rue my shame,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And ban thine enemies, both mine and thine.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Be patient, gentle Nell. Forget this grief.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, Gloucester, teach me to forget myself!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For whilst I think I am thy married wife

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And thou a prince, Protector of this land,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Methinks I should not thus be led along,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Mailed up in shame, with papers on my back,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And followed with a rabble that rejoice

speaker: DUCHESS
text: To see my tears and hear my deep-fet groans.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: The ruthless flint doth cut my tender feet,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And when I start, the envious people laugh

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And bid me be advisèd how I tread.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, Humphrey, can I bear this shameful yoke?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Trowest thou that e’er I’ll look upon the world

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Or count them happy that enjoys the sun?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: No, dark shall be my light, and night my day.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: To think upon my pomp shall be my hell.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Sometimes I’ll say I am Duke Humphrey’s wife

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And he a prince and ruler of the land;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Yet so he ruled and such a prince he was

speaker: DUCHESS
text: As he stood by whilst I, his forlorn duchess,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Was made a wonder and a pointing-stock

speaker: DUCHESS
text: To every idle rascal follower.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But be thou mild, and blush not at my shame,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Nor stir at nothing till the ax of death

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Hang over thee, as, sure, it shortly will.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For Suffolk, he that can do all in all

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With her that hateth thee and hates us all,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And York and impious Beaufort, that false priest,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Have all limed bushes to betray thy wings;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And fly thou how thou canst, they’ll tangle thee.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But fear not thou until thy foot be snared,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Nor never seek prevention of thy foes.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ah, Nell, forbear. Thou aimest all awry.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I must offend before I be attainted;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And had I twenty times so many foes,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And each of them had twenty times their power,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: All these could not procure me any scathe

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: So long as I am loyal, true, and crimeless.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Wouldst have me rescue thee from this reproach?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Why, yet thy scandal were not wiped away,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But I in danger for the breach of law.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thy greatest help is quiet, gentle Nell.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I pray thee, sort thy heart to patience;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: These few days’ wonder will be quickly worn.

speaker: HERALD
text: I summon your Grace to his Majesty’s Parliament

speaker: HERALD
text: Holden at Bury the first of this next month.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And my consent ne’er asked herein before?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This is close dealing. Well, I will be there.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My Nell, I take my leave.—And, master sheriff,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let not her penance exceed the King’s commission.

speaker: SHERIFF
text: An ’t please your Grace, here my commission stays,

speaker: SHERIFF
text: And Sir John Stanley is appointed now

speaker: SHERIFF
text: To take her with him to the Isle of Man.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Must you, Sir John, protect my lady here?

speaker: STANLEY
text: So am I given in charge, may ’t please your Grace.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Entreat her not the worse in that I pray

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You use her well. The world may laugh again,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And I may live to do you kindness, if

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You do it her. And so, Sir John, farewell.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What, gone, my lord, and bid me not farewell?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Witness my tears. I cannot stay to speak.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Art thou gone too? All comfort go with thee,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For none abides with me. My joy is death—

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Death, at whose name I oft have been afeard,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Because I wished this world’s eternity.—

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Stanley, I prithee, go, and take me hence.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I care not whither, for I beg no favor;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Only convey me where thou art commanded.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Why, madam, that is to the Isle of Man,

speaker: STANLEY
text: There to be used according to your state.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That’s bad enough, for I am but reproach.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And shall I, then, be used reproachfully?

speaker: STANLEY
text: Like to a duchess and Duke Humphrey’s lady;

speaker: STANLEY
text: According to that state you shall be used.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Sheriff, farewell, and better than I fare,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Although thou hast been conduct of my shame.

speaker: SHERIFF
text: It is my office; and, madam, pardon me.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ay, ay, farewell. Thy office is discharged.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Come, Stanley, shall we go?

speaker: STANLEY
text: Madam, your penance done, throw off this sheet,

speaker: STANLEY
text: And go we to attire you for our journey.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: My shame will not be shifted with my sheet.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: No, it will hang upon my richest robes

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And show itself, attire me how I can.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Go, lead the way. I long to see my prison.
